title: Redirect Revalidation, DNS, Address Classes, Proxies, and SSRF · Compiler Case: Address Classification

Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Address Classification model · Theory Address Cl…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Address Classification precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Address Classification model · Theory Address Cl…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 8365 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to block unsafe schemes, addresses, redirects, rebinding, proxies, and metadata while retaining causal evidence; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Address Classification model · Theory Address Cl…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Blocking RFC 1918 IPv4 addresses covers private-network SSRF.
